question.content: For synchrotron radiation observed along the tangent direction, compute the observed pulse duration DeltaT for the given numerical parameters. Output exactly one scientific-notation expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a scientific-notation number; write only the value itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Method: beam-sweep timing with arrival-time compression. Track how the narrow radiation cone sweeps across the tangent observer instead of starting from arc-length subtraction.
step_1.key_point: Use beam-window sweep plus flight-time compression.

step_3.content: The observer receives light only while the line of sight stays inside the beaming window. That window has angular width about 2/γ around the instantaneous velocity direction.
step_3.key_point: The pulse starts and ends when the beam axis enters and leaves the tangent line.

step_5.content: The beam axis rotates with orbital angular speed ω=βc/R, so the source emission interval is the sweep angle divided by that speed: Δt_emit=(2/γ)/ω=2R/(βγc).
step_5.key_point: Turn the beaming angle directly into a source-time interval.

step_7.content: The later photon starts closer to the distant observer by x, so the arrival interval is shorter than the emission interval: ΔT=Δt_emit-x/c. With x≈2R/γ and β≈1, this reduces to ΔT=R/(cγ^3), then the given E and B values give the same numerical result as solution1.
step_7.key_point: Arrival compression converts the sweep time into the observed pulse width.
